name: Unstuffed Shells Pasta
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil. Add the pasta shells and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 9 minutes. Drain well in a colander, shaking off excess water, and set aside.

2. Preheat the oven to 180 degrees Celsius. Lightly grease a baking dish with cooking spray or a bit of olive oil.

3. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef, breaking it up with a wooden spoon. Season with salt, Italian seasoning, and black pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the meat is lightly browned and no longer pink, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat if needed.

4. Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet with the beef. Cook, stirring frequently, until the onion softens and becomes translucent, about 5 minutes.

5. Reduce the heat to medium. Stir in the marinara sauce and cook, stirring occasionally, until the sauce begins to simmer and the flavors meld, about 3 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.

6. In a large mixing bowl, combine the ricotta, cream cheese, grated Parmesan, chopped fresh basil, egg, remaining minced garlic, salt, Italian seasoning, and black pepper. Stir with a spatula or wooden spoon until all ingredients are well combined and the mixture is smooth.

7. Add the cooked pasta shells to the cheese mixture. Gently fold them in with a spatula until each shell is coated with the creamy cheese mixture.

8. Spread half of the beef and marinara mixture evenly across the bottom of the prepared baking dish. Spoon the pasta and cheese mixture on top, spreading it into an even layer. Pour the remaining beef mixture over the pasta layer, spreading it to cover. Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese evenly over the top.

9. Cover the dish loosely with aluminum foil to prevent excessive browning. Place in the preheated oven and bake until the casserole is bubbling around the edges and the cheese starts to melt, about 25 minutes. Remove the foil and continue baking until the mozzarella is fully melted and lightly golden on top, about 10 minutes more.

10. Remove from the oven and let rest for a few minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh basil leaves and serve hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook pasta shells until al dente, drain and set aside.

2. Preheat oven and grease baking dish.

3. Brown ground beef with seasonings, then add onion and garlic until softened.

4. Stir in marinara sauce and simmer briefly.

5. Mix cheeses with egg, herbs, and seasonings, then fold in cooked pasta.

6. Layer half the beef mixture in dish, add pasta mixture, top with remaining beef and mozzarella.

7. Bake covered, then uncovered until bubbly and golden.

8. Garnish with basil and serve hot.
